string 2: Prepare for takeoff!
The sea... I wonder how Shellmon is doing.
Oh? You know Shellmon, do you?

Yeah. We've been to the Abyss Area before. Is Shellmon
a friend of yours, too?

You could say that... because the business I mentioned
earlier is with none other than Shellmon!
Is Shellmon doing well these days?{next}
I wonder if we mean the same Shellmon.
So, our friends turned out to be friends with each other...
I should say so!

After all, Shellmon was able to, by herself, rebuild that village
that was once uninhabitable because of the Titans.

I would think so. There weren't any other Shellmon in that
village, after all.

Hee hee. Pretty soon, every Digimon in the Digital World
will be friends!
I'd love it if that could happen...

And thanks to her persistent peace negotiations with the sea
Titans, that village is allowed more autonomy than most!

Shellmon's "talking things out," just like you were hoping,
and it seems to have made the Titans there more peaceful.
Shellmon's trying to protect that village.

As a moderate Titan, I've been meeting with Shellmon in
secret. We get along, as our vision for the future aligns.

The Titans are only interested in the Current. Our talks focus 
on whether the village will relinquish control of it.

As long as the Titans can get their hands on the energy of the 
Current there, the rest must seem like small potatoes.
See? Take a look for yourself.
The ocean... It turned red?
No way! It was blue just eight years ago...

I'd wager the stymied flow of the Current has something to do 
with that... Anyway, we've almost reached the village.
And here we are!

This "Current" Lanamon mentioned... That's a resource of the 
Digital World, if I recall correctly.

And if the conflict started over control of this resource, it 
might be a difficult problem to solve, indeed.

Lots of the conflicts in our world happened over resources.
Any history book will tell you how awful that can get...
I'm heading over to see Shellmon right away. Want to come with?
I say we go with Lanamon.{next}
We ought to gather info on our own.
Let's go swimming in the ocean!

Shellmon left this area once, but I guess she must've come
back, huh?

And she's been negotiating with the Titans all this time...
That's pretty awesome.

That means she gets that fighting only robs people of what
they love. I think Shellmon might be our best hope.

Not a bad idea, sure... Still, I think we should go talk to
Shellmon directly.

I was looking forward to seeing the Digital World's ocean
again after all this time, but...
...a red ocean? That's just plain weird.

We ought to talk to a Digimon who can tell us what
happened to it.

Yes. Call it a hunch, but I'd say that'll help you cut to the heart 
of the matter quickly.
All right, then. Let's head on over!

It doesn't look like there are any Titans to be found. I bet
it's all thanks to Shellmon's hard work.
Still, everyone looks so depressed...
We'd better ask Shellmon what's going on around here.
Yeah. Let's hurry.
What's the matter? Is something wrong?
It's just that... it feels so strange to see Shellmon again...

For Shellmon, it's been eight years since we last saw each
other, right? I wonder if she recognizes us...

It was a sad goodbye back then, so I hope we can have a
good time chatting now.
Hey, Shellmon, you've got visitors!
You're the only one who still calls me that, you know.
Now, as for my guests...
W-Wait. Whaaat?

Hold on... You mean, you're Shellmon? Same as
the one from back then?
I can hardly believe this... Yes, I remember all of you!
...It seems you've all been through some things as well.
I go by "Venusmon" now.

This Digimon you seek, well-versed in the healing
arts... I think they're referring to me.

Didn't I predict you could cut to the heart of the matter quickly? 
I had a feeling this was the one you were seeking!
Digimon come from all around for her healing touch.
Even Titans come here for help... Titans like me, for instance.
I can act this way despite being a Titan...

...because you nursed me back to health. And you
didn't care who I was, or where I came from.

That takes me back... You're the only one who
still calls me that, you know.

Venusmon, we need your help. Merukimon is in the human
world, and is hurt—pretty badly, from the sound of it.
That is... quite concerning, indeed...

Still, the very idea of leaving here is simply
unfathomable... not with things as they stand now.

Though we have a temporary ceasefire, the period
we agreed on will soon come to an end.
In that case, this might solve the problem.

Here it is! Remember this little beauty you asked me for? 
A present as a token of peace, I believe you were thinking?
All that remains is to give it to the Titans.
And I've got an idea! Why not ask these folks to deliver it?
The other Titans and I don't exactly see eye to eye, after all...
...but you folks are good at talking to anyone—friend or foe!

Besides, I imagine you could handle anything
that might pop up along the way as well!
So, what should we do?
Okay, let's take the request.{next}
That's not something to be taken on lightly.
What if Venusmon came with us?
Okay, let's go!

Are you sure that's a good idea? If we don't go,
then Venusmon will probably have to go instead.
And if Venusmon gets hurt, who's gonna heal Merukimon?
Good point. Best to avoid that situation, if at all possible.
But... what if something happens to Venusmon?

Who'd heal Merukimon then?
I don't think that's such a good idea...

The Titans around here won't be a problem. Or shouldn't be,
anyway. The temporary ceasefire's still in place, after all.
All right, then. We're off, Venusmon.
Wait, Inori!
What is it, Aegiomon?
Gwaaaaaaargh!
Why, you—!
Gwaaargh...!
A Titan?! But what about the ceasefire?!

Doesn't look like it from where I'm standing... I don't
think we have much choice!
GWAAAAARGH...!

*panting* C'mon guys... We'd better hurry. I bet there are more
of 'em around here.
Looks like talking alone won't be enough, will it...
Who are you? You do not appear to be Titans...

The village sent us. We came to deliver this present, as a
token of peace.

Wait. I remember you! It's me, Divermon! The one you met
eight years ago!
Divermon?!
Don't tell me you're the leader of the Titans here?

Yep, I am, under the name of "Neptunemon." To think we
would meet again, after all this time...!
This token of peace... Rest assured that I have received it gladly.

I don't want to fight with the village at all. I sent a few
envoys to negotiate but...

...they just left me hanging, even as the deadline was
approaching. I was wondering what was going on...
Uh-oh... You don't suppose...?

Yeah... That that thing that attacked us had something to do
with that...?

What do you intend to do now? If you are heading back to the
village, I would like to ask a favor of you.

Is that the scarf Shellmon wrapped your wound with?! I can't
believe you still have it after all this time!
I want you to return this scarf to Shellmon. And to deliver this message:

"I owe you my life. I'll do everything in my power to
repay your kindness."
Got it! We'll give Shellmon the message for sure.
Thanks, I'm counting on you.

Hey, Venusmon! We delivered the token of peace. And we
brought you back an amazing souvenir!
You brought something back for me, you say?
Oh! Th-That scarf...!

We handed the bottle of wine over to the Titan leader, and
that leader turned out to be the Divermon you saved!

Divermon was thrilled with the token of peace, and has been
wanting to renew the reconciliation with your village!
I bet this will change everything! Don't you think?

Oh, that's right! Divermon—he's "Neptunemon" now—had a
message for you, too!

"I owe you my life. I shall do everything in my power to
repay your kindness," he says!
And this is for you, from Neptunemon!

Are you all right...? Maybe we should give Venusmon a
little privacy, guys.

We'll be right outside. Give us a shout if you need
anything, okay?
What... have I done...?!
A token of peace...
Shellmon...
*gulping*
Wh-What is this...?
In the end... I never got to tell you...
...how grateful I am...
...for what you did back then...
Shellmon...
Th-The ocean is growing even redder than before...

But how could that be? Neptunemon should have finished
drinking the poisoned wine by now...!

No...! Could this sullying of the ocean water... have been
the work of someone other than the Titans all along...?
How... could I have been so wrong...?!
